post.summary: Upsert a directory adapter and optionally activate it.
post.description:
This handler performs directory analysis and adapter registration with proper async/blocking separation.

# Blocking Operations

All blocking operations (filesystem I/O and CPU-intensive analysis) are executed in a dedicated
blocking thread pool via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` to prevent head-of-line blocking on the
async runtime. The handler combines three phases into a single blocking call:

1. **Path Validation**: Validates root path exists and relative path is safe (no `..`)
2. **Directory Analysis**: CPU-intensive codebase analysis with `adapteros_codegraph`
3. **Artifact Creation**: Generates deterministic `.safetensors` bytes and persists artifact

# Timeout Protection

The combined blocking operation is wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout` with a configurable duration
(default: 120 seconds, configured via `ApiConfig::directory_analysis_timeout_secs`). This prevents
malicious or extremely large directories from tying up blocking threads indefinitely.

# Error Handling

Errors are returned with appropriate HTTP status codes:
- `400 BAD_REQUEST`: Invalid paths, path traversal attempts, or analysis failures
- `408 REQUEST_TIMEOUT`: Operation exceeded configured timeout
- `500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`: Filesystem errors or task panics

# Observability

The handler includes structured tracing spans for each phase:
- `directory_adapter_blocking_ops`: Top-level span for entire blocking operation
- `path_validation`: Path validation phase
- `directory_analysis`: Directory analysis phase (includes root and path fields)
- `artifact_creation`: Artifact write phase (includes content hash field)

# Permissions

Requires `Admin` or `Operator` role via RBAC.

# Example

```no_run
POST /v1/adapters/directory/upsert
{
  "root": "/workspace/my-project",
  "path": "src",
  "tenant_id": "tenant-a",
  "activate": false
}
```

put.description:
This endpoint allows re-uploading a chunk that may have failed or been corrupted.
Unlike the regular upload_chunk endpoint, this allows overwriting an existing chunk.
Optionally, the client can provide an expected hash for validation.

## Use Cases
- Network failure during chunk upload
- Chunk corruption detected during validation
- Resume after partial failure

## Error Cases
- 404: Session not found or expired
- 400: Invalid chunk index or hash mismatch
- 413: Chunk size exceeds the session's configured chunk size
- 500: Failed to write chunk to disk

parameters.name: chunk_index

post.summary: Upload a single chunk for a chunked upload session
post.description:
This endpoint receives a single chunk of data for an ongoing chunked upload.
Chunks can be uploaded in any order and the system will track which chunks
have been received. The session must have been initiated first with the
initiate_chunked_upload endpoint.

## Error Cases
- 404: Session not found or expired (sessions expire after 24 hours)
- 400: Invalid chunk index (negative or exceeds expected chunks)
- 409: Chunk already uploaded (duplicate chunk index)
- 413: Chunk size exceeds the session's configured chunk size
- 500: Failed to write chunk to disk

post.summary: Complete a chunked upload and create the dataset
post.description:
This endpoint assembles all uploaded chunks into the final file and creates
a dataset entry in the database. All chunks must have been uploaded before
calling this endpoint.

## Cleanup Strategy
- On success: Temporary chunk files are deleted during assembly
- On failure: Temporary files remain for retry; session expires after 24 hours
- Abandoned sessions: Manual cleanup marks expired sessions failed and removes temp files
  (see UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS in chunked_upload.rs)

400.description: Upload not complete or validation failed

post.summary: Create a training dataset from selected chat messages
post.description:
Converts chat messages into JSONL training format based on the specified format.
Supports multi-turn conversation format, instruction-response extraction, and raw preservation.

The JSONL format depends on the `format` field:
- `conversation`: Multi-turn format with `{"messages": [{"role": "...", "content": "..."}]}`
- `instruction_response`: Extract user/assistant pairs as `{"prompt": "...", "completion": "..."}`
- `raw`: Each message as `{"role": "...", "content": "..."}`

post.summary: Create a training dataset from existing documents or a document collection
post.description:
Converts RAG documents into JSONL training format. Either `document_id` or
`collection_id` must be provided (mutually exclusive). The resulting dataset
is immediately marked as valid since the source documents are already indexed.

The JSONL format is: `{"text": "<chunk_text>"}` for each chunk, ordered
deterministically by (document_id ASC, chunk_index ASC) for reproducibility.

400.description: Invalid request - must provide exactly one of document_id or collection_id

post.summary: Create a training dataset from pasted or extracted text
post.description:
Converts raw text content into JSONL training format based on the specified format.
Supports line-by-line processing, Q&A extraction, and raw text preservation.

The JSONL format depends on the `format` field:
- `lines`: Each non-empty line becomes `{"text": "<line>"}`
- `qa`: Attempts to parse Q:/A: pairs into `{"prompt": "...", "completion": "..."}`
- `raw`: Single entry with full content as `{"text": "<content>"}`

post.summary: Synthesize training data from document chunks
post.description:
Takes document chunks and uses a local synthesis model to generate
structured training examples (Q&A pairs, instructions, completions).

The synthesis model runs locally on Apple Silicon using CoreML/ANE
for efficient inference.

get.summary: Stream dataset upload and processing progress via SSE
get.description:
This endpoint establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection that streams
progress events for dataset operations. Clients can connect to receive real-time
updates about:
- File upload progress (percentage, current file)
- Dataset validation progress (files processed, validation results)
- Statistics computation progress

Events are JSON objects with the following fields:
- `dataset_id`: The ID of the dataset being processed
- `event_type`: One of "upload", "validation", or "statistics"
- `current_file`: The file currently being processed (optional)
- `percentage_complete`: Overall progress as a percentage (0-100)
- `total_files`: Total number of files in the dataset (optional)
- `files_processed`: Number of files processed so far (optional)
- `message`: Human-readable status message
- `timestamp`: RFC3339 formatted timestamp

Example client usage (JavaScript):
```javascript
const eventSource = new EventSource('/v1/datasets/upload/progress?dataset_id=abc123');
eventSource.onmessage = (event) => {
  const progress = JSON.parse(event.data);
  console.log(`${progress.message}: ${progress.percentage_complete}%`);
};
```

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of dataset progress

Process a document: parse, chunk, generate embeddings, and index for RAG
post.description:
This endpoint takes an uploaded document and:
1. Parses the document content (PDF/Markdown)
2. Chunks the content with configurable parameters
3. Generates embeddings for each chunk
4. Stores chunks in document_chunks table (for evidence tracking)
5. Indexes chunks in rag_documents table (for vector search)
6. Updates document status to "indexed"

**IMPORTANT**: This establishes the unified ID format where:
- `document_chunks.id` is a UUID (used for FK in inference_evidence)
- `rag_documents.doc_id` is `{document_id}__chunk_{index}` using the document's UUID
- This allows collection filtering to work correctly with UUID-based document_ids

post.description:
Accepts inference requests and returns a stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE)
with tokens as they are generated. Compatible with OpenAI's streaming API.

# SSE Event Format
```text
data: {"id":"chatcmpl-xxx","object":"chat.completion.chunk","choices":[{"delta":{"content":"token"}}]}
data: [DONE]
```

# Collection-Scoped RAG
When `collection_id` is provided, the inference will be scoped to documents
within that collection, enabling targeted RAG retrieval for domain-specific queries.

# Example
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Hello, world!", "max_tokens": 100}'
```

# Example with Collection-Scoped RAG
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "What is the licensing model?", "max_tokens": 200, "collection_id": "marketing-docs"}'
```

post.summary: Streaming inference handler with loading progress
post.description:
Accepts inference requests and returns a stream of Server-Sent Events (SSE)
with model loading progress, then inference tokens. This endpoint automatically
detects if the requested adapter needs to be loaded and streams progress events.

# SSE Event Format
```text
data: {"event":"Loading","phase":"LoadingWeights","progress":0,"eta_seconds":30}
data: {"event":"Loading","phase":"Warmup","progress":50,"eta_seconds":10}
data: {"event":"Ready","warmup_latency_ms":1234}
data: {"event":"Token","text":"Hello","token_id":null}
data: {"event":"Done","total_tokens":10,"latency_ms":5678}
```

# Example
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/infer/stream/progress \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
  -d '{"prompt": "Hello, world!", "max_tokens": 100, "adapters": ["my-adapter"]}'
```

post.summary: Import a model from a path on disk
post.description:
# Endpoint
POST /v1/models/import

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
Requires one of: Admin, Operator

# Request Body
- `model_name`: Name for the imported model
- `model_path`: Filesystem path to model directory
- `format`: Model format (mlx, safetensors, pytorch, gguf)
- `backend`: Backend to use (mlx, metal, coreml)
- `capabilities`: Optional list of capabilities (chat, completion, embeddings)
- `metadata`: Optional JSON metadata

# Response
Returns import status with:
- `import_id`: Unique identifier for the imported model
- `status`: Import status (available, in_progress, failed)
- `message`: Human-readable status message
- `progress`: Import progress percentage (0-100)

The import process:
1. Validates path exists and format is supported
2. Scans directory and computes BLAKE3 hashes for all files
3. Detects model structure and configuration
4. Registers model in database with metadata

# Errors
- `FORBIDDEN` (403): User lacks required role
- `BAD_REQUEST` (400): Invalid path, unsupported format, or unsupported backend
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Import failure, database error

# Example
```
POST /v1/models/import
{
  "model_name": "qwen-7b",
  "model_path": "/var/model-cache/models/qwen2.5-7b-instruct-bf16",
  "format": "mlx",
  "backend": "mlx",
  "capabilities": ["chat", "completion"]
}
```

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/status

# Authentication
Optional - unauthenticated requests receive limited response

# Permissions (when authenticated)
Requires one of: Operator, Admin, Compliance

# Query Parameters
- `tenant_id`: Optional tenant ID filter (defaults to "default", only applies when authenticated)

# Response
Returns the current base model load status. Response varies by authentication:

**Unauthenticated response (limited data):**
- `model_id`: "none"
- `model_name`: "No Model Loaded"
- `model_path`: null
- `status`: "unloaded"
- `loaded_at`: null
- `unloaded_at`: null
- `error_message`: null
- `memory_usage_mb`: null
- `is_loaded`: false
- `updated_at`: Current timestamp

**Authenticated response (full data):**
- `model_id`: Identifier of the loaded model (or "none")
- `model_name`: Human-readable model name (or "No Model Loaded")
- `model_path`: Filesystem path to model files
- `status`: Load status (loaded, unloaded, loading, error)
- `loaded_at`: Timestamp when model was loaded
- `unloaded_at`: Timestamp when model was unloaded
- `error_message`: Error message if status is error
- `memory_usage_mb`: Memory consumption in MB
- `is_loaded`: Boolean flag indicating if model is currently in memory
- `updated_at`: Last status update timestamp

# Errors
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model referenced in status record not found in database (authenticated only)
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure (authenticated only)

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/status?tenant_id=default
```

# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/status/all

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
Requires one of: Operator, Admin, Compliance

# Query Parameters
- `tenant_id`: Optional tenant filter to show only models for specific tenant

# Response
Returns aggregated status for all base models:
- `schema_version`: API schema version
- `models`: Array of model status records with:
  - `model_id`: Model identifier
  - `model_name`: Model name
  - `model_path`: Filesystem path
  - `status`: Load status (loaded, unloaded, loading, error)
  - `loaded_at`: Load timestamp
  - `unloaded_at`: Unload timestamp
  - `error_message`: Error message if status is error
  - `memory_usage_mb`: Memory consumption in MB
  - `is_loaded`: Boolean flag
  - `updated_at`: Last status update
- `total_memory_mb`: Total memory used by all loaded models
- `available_memory_mb`: Available memory (currently null)
- `active_model_count`: Number of currently loaded models

# Errors
- `FORBIDDEN` (403): User lacks required role
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/status/all?tenant_id=default
```

get.description:
# Endpoint
GET /v1/models/{model_id}/validate

# Authentication
Required

# Permissions
All authenticated users

# Response
Validates model integrity by checking stored BLAKE3 hashes. Returns:
- `model_id`: Model identifier
- `status`: Validation status (ready, invalid)
- `valid`: Boolean indicating if all hashes are valid
- `can_load`: Boolean indicating if model can be loaded
- `reason`: Description of validation failure (if any)
- `issues`: List of validation issues with type and message
- `errors`: Legacy field for backwards compatibility

Validates:
- Model weights file hash (BLAKE3)
- Config file hash (BLAKE3)
- Tokenizer files hashes (BLAKE3)
- Metadata JSON format

This is a logical validation (hash comparison) and does not require actual file access.

# Errors
- `NOT_FOUND` (404): Model does not exist in database
- `INTERNAL_ERROR` (500): Database query failure

# Example
```
GET /v1/models/qwen-7b/validate
```

post.summary: Register a worker with manifest binding
post.description:
Workers call this endpoint on startup to register with the control plane.
The control plane validates:
1. The plan exists
2. The worker's manifest_hash matches the plan's expected manifest_hash_b3
3. The schema_version is compatible

On successful registration, the worker is inserted with status 'registered'.
The worker must then call the status notification endpoint to transition to 'healthy'.

**Note:** This endpoint is called by workers, not by users. No auth required.

# Example
```
POST /v1/workers/register
{
  "worker_id": "01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef",
  "tenant_id": "tenant-123",
  "plan_id": "plan-456",
  "manifest_hash": "abc123...",
  "schema_version": "1.0",
  "api_version": "1.0",
  "pid": 12345,
  "uds_path": "/var/run/aos/tenant-123/worker.sock",
  "capabilities": ["coreml", "mlx"]
}
```

Workers call this endpoint to notify the control plane of status changes.
This uses validated state transitions.

**Note:** This endpoint is called by workers, not by users. No auth required.

# Example
```
POST /v1/workers/status
{
  "worker_id": "01234567-89ab-cdef-0123-456789abcdef",
  "status": "serving",
  "reason": "model loaded successfully"
}
```

Drain a worker (transition to draining state without SIGTERM)
post.description:
Gracefully drains a worker by transitioning it to the 'draining' state.
Unlike `stop_worker`, this does NOT send SIGTERM to the process.
The worker continues processing in-flight requests and should eventually
transition to 'stopped' via the status notification endpoint.

**Use case:** UI-initiated drain where the operator wants to stop new requests
but allow current work to complete before deciding whether to stop the process.

**Permissions:** Requires `WorkerManage` permission (Operator or Admin role).

**Telemetry:** Emits `worker.drain` event.

# Example
```
POST /v1/workers/{worker_id}/drain
```

# State Fields

This type exposes two distinct state concepts:

- `lifecycle_state`: The adapter's lifecycle phase in the release workflow.
  See [`LifecycleState`] for the canonical set of values.

- `runtime_state`: The adapter's current memory/runtime status.
  Values: "unloaded", "cold", "warm", "hot", "resident"
  This reflects whether the adapter is loaded and at what priority tier.

Note: In the database, `current_state` maps to `runtime_state` in the API
to provide clearer semantics.

OpenAiChatCompletionsResponse.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completions response.

## Receipt Digest Extraction

The `receipt_digest` from the underlying inference is embedded in this response:
- **`id`**: Format `chatcmpl_{receipt_digest_hex_prefix}` (first 16 hex chars).
- **`system_fingerprint`**: The full 64-character hex-encoded receipt digest.

Clients can extract the receipt digest for verification/audit by:
1. Using `system_fingerprint` directly (full hex digest)
2. Looking up the receipt via `/v1/runs/{run_id}/evidence` endpoint

system_fingerprint.description:
System fingerprint for determinism tracking (full receipt_digest as hex).

This is the BLAKE3 hash of the run receipt, allowing clients to:
- Verify deterministic replay capability
- Look up the full evidence chain via `/v1/runs/{run_id}/evidence`
System fingerprint for determinism tracking (full receipt_digest as hex).

RoutingPolicy.description:
Routing policy configuration

Minimal, deterministic constraints applied after router scoring but
before kernels run. Designed to express per-tenant/workspace adapter
allowlists/denylists and an optional per-token adapter cap without
changing router math.

allowed_adapter_ids.description:
Restrict routing to specific adapter IDs.
When present, only these adapters may be routed.
When None/null, all adapters are allowed.

allowed_clusters.description:
Restrict routing to specific clusters (semantic grouping of adapters).
When present, only these clusters may be used.

allowed_stack_ids.description:
Restrict routing to specific stack IDs.
When present, only these stacks may be used.
When None/null, all stacks are allowed.

cluster_fallback.description:
Behavior when a cluster transition is blocked.
- stay_on_current: remain on current adapters
- fallback_to_base: route to base-only

denied_adapter_ids.description:
Explicitly deny routing to specific adapter IDs.
Takes precedence over allowlist when both are provided.

denied_clusters.description: Explicitly deny routing to specific clusters.

max_adapters_per_token.description:
Maximum number of adapters allowed per token after routing policy is applied.
When None/null, defaults to the router's K value.

max_reasoning_depth.description:
Maximum number of cluster transitions (hops) allowed per request.
When exceeded, router applies cluster_fallback behavior.

pin_enforcement.description:
How to handle pins outside the effective routing set.
"warn": Log warning but allow inference (default)
"error": Reject inference request

require_pins.description:
Require pinned adapters to be specified.
When true, requests without pinned_adapter_ids are rejected.

require_stack.description:
Require a stack to be specified in inference requests.
When true, requests without stack_id are rejected.

StopPolicySpec.description:
Stop policy specification for deterministic stopping behavior.

Configures thresholds and parameters for the stop controller.
The policy is hashed (BLAKE3) and committed to the receipt for audit.

completion_threshold_q15.description:
Q15 threshold for EOS probability triggering COMPLETION_CONFIDENT.
Range: 0-32767 where 32767 = 1.0 probability.
Default: 24576 (~0.75)

eos_token_id.description: Optional explicit EOS token ID (uses model default if not specified)

output_max_tokens.description: Hard cap on output tokens (overrides request max_tokens if lower)

repetition_ngram.description:
Minimum n-gram size for repetition detection.
The detector scans all n-gram sizes from this minimum to window_size/2.
Default: 3

repetition_threshold.description:
Threshold count for repetition detection. If any n-gram in the window
appears more than this many times, repetition is flagged.
Default: 1 (triggers when any n-gram appears > 1 time, i.e., 2+ occurrences)

repetition_window.description:
Sliding window size for repetition detection in tokens.
Default: 32

stop_sequences.description: Explicit stop sequences to terminate generation (matched on tokenized output).

StopReasonCode.description:
Exhaustive stop reason codes for inference termination.

This enum forms a complete partition of all possible termination states:
- Normal completion: Length, BudgetMax, CompletionConfident, RepetitionGuard, StopSequence
- Abnormal termination: Cancelled, SystemError

Every generation run MUST terminate with one of these codes, which is then
bound into the receipt for verifiable proof of why generation ended.

fallback_reason.description: Reason for falling back (e.g., coreml_unavailable, policy_fallback).

ready.description: Whether training can start with the resolved backend.

resolved_backend.description: Backend the system will actually use based on availability/policy.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings (e.g., silent fallback, missing ANE/GPU).

TrainingBaseModelReadiness.description: Base model lifecycle/retry hints derived from BaseModelState persistence.

error_message.description: Error surfaced by the worker for the latest lifecycle transition.

model_id.description: Model identifier if a base model is registered.

retry_exhausted.description: Whether automatic retries have been exhausted.

TrainingConfigRequest.description:
Training configuration request (WASM-compatible)

This type is used by both UI (WASM) and server contexts. Server-only fields
(e.g., `coreml_placement`, `base_model_path`, `preprocessing`) are only available
with the `server` feature.

backend_policy.description: Backend policy when CoreML is preferred (coreml_only/coreml_else_fallback/auto)

base_model_path.description: Path to base model for training (server-only)

coreml_placement.description: Optional CoreML placement spec for training/export alignment (server-only)

coreml_training_fallback.description: Explicit fallback when CoreML is requested and unavailable

early_stopping.description: Enable early stopping when validation loss stops improving.

enable_coreml_export.description: Opt-in CoreML export after successful training

force_resume.description: Force resume even when pipeline/checkpoint compatibility checks fail.

max_gpu_memory_mb.description: Maximum GPU memory in MB (best-effort, 0/unset = unlimited)

min_delta.description: Minimum loss change to qualify as an improvement (requires early_stopping).

multi_module_training.description: Enable multi-module training (train separate weights per target module).

patience.description: Number of epochs without improvement before stopping (requires early_stopping).

preferred_backend.description: Optional GPU backend preference (coreml, mlx, metal, cpu)

oneOf.description: Optional CoreML preprocessing stage configuration (server-only)

require_gpu.description: Require GPU acceleration (error if no GPU backend can be initialized)

validation_split.description: Fraction of dataset to use for validation (0.0-0.5).

TrainingCoremlReadiness.description: CoreML readiness state, including compute units chosen after fallback.

TrainingExportAdapter.description: Adapter metadata for training provenance export

chunking_config.description: Chunking configuration used for document processing

TrainingExportJob.description: Training job reference for provenance export

base_model_id.description: Base model ID used for training

oneOf.description: Dataset used for training

adapter_id.description: Adapter ID after packaging (populated on completion)

adapter_repo_id.description: Alias of `repo_id` for clarity in clients that treat this as an adapter repo identifier.

artifact_hash_b3.description:
Alias of `package_hash_b3` for clients expecting an "artifact hash" surface.

**Deprecated since 1.0.0**: Use `package_hash_b3` instead.
- **Removal timeline**: Will be removed in version 2.0.0 (see [`DEPRECATED_FIELD_SUNSET_VERSION`]).
- **Migration**: Replace `artifact_hash_b3` references with `package_hash_b3`.
- Both fields currently return the same value for backward compatibility.

# OpenAPI
This field is marked deprecated in the OpenAPI schema.

artifact_path.description:
Alias of `aos_path` for clients expecting an "artifact path" surface.

**Deprecated since 1.0.0**: Use `aos_path` instead.
- **Removal timeline**: Will be removed in version 2.0.0 (see [`DEPRECATED_FIELD_SUNSET_VERSION`]).
- **Migration**: Replace `artifact_path` references with `aos_path`.
- Both fields currently return the same value for backward compatibility.

# OpenAPI
This field is marked deprecated in the OpenAPI schema.

backend.description: Actual backend used for training (resolved from request + availability).

base_model_id.description: Base model ID used for training

collection_id.description: Document collection ID used for training

config_hash_b3.description: BLAKE3 hash of training config for reproducibility

coreml_fusion_verified.description:
Whether the CoreML export used fusion verification.
If false, the package may be stub/metadata-only and not production-ready.

coreml_training_fallback.description: Explicit fallback backend when CoreML is unavailable (mlx/metal).

correlation_id.description: Correlation ID for tracing dataset -> training -> inference

current_epoch.description:
Current training epoch (0-indexed).
None if progress data is not yet available.

# Serialization Behavior
- On serialization: Omitted when `None` (via `skip_serializing_if`)
- On deserialization: Absent fields deserialize to `None` (via `#[serde(default)]`)

current_loss.description:
Current training loss value.
None if progress data is not yet available.

# Serialization Behavior
- On serialization: Omitted when `None` (via `skip_serializing_if`)
- On deserialization: Absent fields deserialize to `None` (via `#[serde(default)]`)

dataset_hash_b3.description: Dataset manifest hash captured at job creation (combined when multi-dataset).

dataset_version_ids.description: Dataset versions used for this job (order preserved)

dataset_version_trust.description: Trust snapshot for dataset versions at training start

display_name.description:
Human-readable display name derived from the job's typed ID word alias.
Example: "job-swift-moon". Populated when the ID uses the TypedId format.

error_code.description:
Structured error code for failed jobs (e.g., "TRAIN_E101_GPU_OOM").
Use `aosctl explain <code>` for remediation guidance.

oneOf.description:
Marketing/operational tier for routing and UI badges (micro/standard/max)

# OpenAPI
Uses proper enum schema with values: `micro`, `standard`, `max`.

manifest_hash_source.description: Indicates the source of manifest_hash_b3: "manifest" or "package_fallback"

progress_pct.description:
Training progress percentage (0.0-100.0).
None if progress data is not yet available (distinguishes from 0% progress).

# Serialization Behavior
- On serialization: Omitted when `None` (via `skip_serializing_if`)
- On deserialization: Absent fields deserialize to `None` (via `#[serde(default)]`)

scope.description: Logical scope for adapter visibility (e.g., project, tenant)

stack_id.description: Stack ID for adapter stack association

status.description: Current training status (pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled)

tokens_per_second.description:
Tokens processed per second.
None if progress data is not yet available.

# Serialization Behavior
- On serialization: Omitted when `None` (via `skip_serializing_if`)
- On deserialization: Absent fields deserialize to `None` (via `#[serde(default)]`)

warnings.description:
Warning messages related to training or export.
Populated when there are non-fatal issues that users should be aware of.

weights_hash_b3.description: BLAKE3 hash of adapter weights (for verification)

TrainingMetricEntry.description:
Individual training metric entry for time-series data

Note: `learning_rate` is optional because per-step LR is not currently stored
in the training_metrics table. It's available at job level via TrainingProgress.
`tokens_processed` is populated when available from the "tokens_processed" metric.

learning_rate.description: Learning rate at this step (optional - not stored per-step in current schema)

tokens_processed.description: Tokens processed up to this point

TrainingMetricsListResponse.description: Training metrics list response for time-series metrics endpoint

TrainingProvenanceExportResponse.description:
Complete training provenance export response

Returns full provenance data for an adapter including:
- Adapter metadata (id, name, version, base_model)
- Training jobs that produced this adapter
- Datasets used for training
- Documents with their content hashes
- Configuration versions (chunking, training)
- Export timestamp and integrity hash

export_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of the entire export for integrity verification

schema_version.description: Schema version for this response format

training_jobs.description: Training jobs that contributed to this adapter

TrainingQueueJobSummary.description: Summary of a job in the training queue

TrainingQueueResponse.description:
Response for GET /v1/training/queue

Returns the current training queue status including counts by status
and estimated wait times.

avg_training_duration_secs.description: Average training duration for running jobs in seconds (0 if no running jobs)

avg_wait_time_secs.description: Average wait time for pending jobs in seconds (0 if no pending jobs)

max_wait_time_secs.description: Oldest pending job's wait time in seconds (None if no pending jobs)

pending_count.description: Number of jobs waiting to start

pending_jobs.description: Summary of pending jobs (limited to first 10)

queue_depth.description: Total number of jobs in the queue (pending + running)

TrainingReportCurves.description: Curve metrics captured in a training report.

TrainingReportMetricDefinitions.description: Metric definitions to avoid "mystery numbers" in reports.

TrainingReportResponse.description: Training report response for report artifact retrieval.

TrainingReportSummary.description: Summary metrics captured in a training report.

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline identifier for this training run.

split_hash.description: Hash of the dataset split definition.

UiConfigResponse.description: Public UI configuration for runtime clients.

UiProfile.description: UI profile for navigation and surface filtering.

UmaMemorySummary.description:
UMA headroom summary.

When `availability` is `Unavailable`, all numeric fields will be `None`.
The UI must display "Unavailable" rather than zeros in this case.

availability.description: Whether UMA metrics are actually available

available_mb.description: Available UMA memory in MB (None if unavailable)

total_mb.description: Total UMA memory in MB (None if unavailable)

used_mb.description: Used UMA memory in MB (None if unavailable)

UpdateErrorAlertRuleRequest.description: Request to update an error alert rule

UpdateExecutionPolicyRequest.description:
Request to update an existing execution policy

All fields are optional to support partial updates.
Fields not provided will preserve existing values.

oneOf.description: Determinism policy configuration (replaces existing if provided)

Golden verification policy configuration (replaces existing if provided)
Use null to remove golden policy.

PRD-ART-01: Require Ed25519 signatures on imported .aos adapter files.
When provided, updates the require_signed_adapters setting.

oneOf.description:
Routing policy configuration (replaces existing if provided)
Use null to remove routing policy.

UpdateRouterWeightsRequest.description: Request to update router feature importance weights

UpdateRoutingRuleParams.description: Parameters for updating an existing routing rule

UpdateStackRequest.description: Request to update an existing adapter stack

UpdateTrainingPriorityRequest.description: Request to update training job priority

UpdateTrainingPriorityResponse.description: Response after updating training job priority

UploadChunkQuery.description: Query parameters for uploading a chunk

training_job_id.description: Training job ID if auto_train was enabled

UploadSessionStatusResponse.description: Response for getting upload session status

received_chunk_indices.description: List of chunk indices that have been received

UploadSessionSummary.description: Summary of an upload session for listing

age_seconds.description: Age of the session in seconds

latest_revision.description: Latest revision number in lineage (if extending)

oneOf.description: Parsed adapter name components (if valid)

violations.description: List of validation violations (empty if valid)

oneOf.description: Parsed stack name components (if valid)

violations.description: List of validation violations (empty if valid)

inference_id.description: The inference ID this verdict applies to

Worker heartbeat request

Worker heartbeat response

WorkerModelLoadState.description:
Worker-specific model load state for smarter routing decisions

Enables the control plane to route requests to workers with models already loaded,
avoiding cold-start latency and memory pressure from concurrent model loads.

Note: This is distinct from `ModelLoadStatus` in `model_status.rs` which is used
for model management APIs. This type is specifically for worker runtime state.

WorkerRegistrationRequest.description:
Worker registration request

Sent by worker on startup to register with the control plane.
The control plane validates manifest_hash against the plan's expected manifest.

active_model_id.description: Active model identifier at registration time (for restart continuity)

backend.description: Backend selected by worker (e.g., mlx, metal, coreml)

oneOf.description: Structured worker capabilities (backend kind + supported modes)

manifest_hash.description: BLAKE3 hash of the loaded manifest (hex-encoded)

model_hash.description: Base model hash advertised by worker

plan_id.description: Plan ID this worker was spawned from

strict_mode.description: Whether worker is running in strict mode (fail-closed on errors)

tokenizer_vocab_size.description: Tokenizer vocabulary size observed by the worker

uds_path.description: Unix domain socket path for this worker

worker_id.description: Unique worker ID (UUID v7, generated by worker)
